parsed_review.summary: CP00-941 is a descriptor-only HRX evidence bridge (EmployeeFixture tail rows RP30.P05.M07.S04-S08 plus CandidateFixture foundation rows RP30.P05.M08.S01-S05, 10 units, risk A). Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is supported and not overclaimed: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, descriptor_only=true, status in_progress with pack_level_claude_review pending_hardened_review. Scope/binding (HRX_CP941_PACK_BINDING, registry.js:831) matches manifest, baseline, and prompt (next CP00-942 / RP30.P05.M08.S06). Coverage matches micro_title_distribution (EmployeeFixture 5, CandidateFixture 5). Descriptor-only/no-runtime/no-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced consistently across the row generator (registry.js:3337), createHrxCp941 descriptor (registry.js:9759), validators (validators.js:7707-7888), the standalone validate script (scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs, which chains all upstream bindings), and contracts/hrx-people-contract.json (latest_projection HrxCp941EmployeeCandidateFixtureTailBridgeDescriptor, 10 rows with correct entity_name markers, validation.valid=true, no_write_attestation all false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false). Tests in packages/hrx/test/model.test.js assert empty validation errors for binding, coverage, descriptor, and contract projection. README CP00-941 entry accurately states the descriptor-only closed boundaries with no overclaim. Authority boundary is intact: Claude is read-only and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), human approval required for runtime opening, enterprise trust closed; Hermes packet is deterministic_only with emits_runtime_receipt=false (review/evidence handling, not product runtime). Changed-file-scope is respected: active implementation refs are consistent and the unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room data/html, the .docx, workbook docs) are correctly excluded, with no omitted active untracked implementation files. Review-evidence hardening checks pass: auth preflight succeeded (exit 0, well-formed JSON, 1228-byte stdout, no 0-byte/malformed/tool-call-shaped output), prompt is 2400 bytes (< 25600 limit), and forbidden tools are absent. No P0/P1/P2 issues. One P3 provenance note only: the closeout artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, command-evidence.json) are still PENDING by design and command-evidence currently records only the 5 HRX-specific commands at exit 0 (gate_outcome pending_final_validation); the broader validation ladder was not re-executed in this read-only receipt. Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; this review does not block pack or this-CP goal closeout.

findings.title: Descriptor correctness verified statically; full closeout validation ladder not re-executed in this read-only receipt
findings.details: command-evidence.json records 5 HRX-specific commands (node --check x3, node --test packages/hrx/test/*.test.js, npm run rp30:hrx:validate) all at exit_code 0, with gate_outcome and status 'pending_final_validation' and hermes_gate.status 'pending'. The broader validation ladder named in the prompt/baseline (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, npm run validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is expected-before-closeout but not yet captured as executed. As a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob, no Bash) I confirmed descriptor/coverage/validator/contract/test correctness and all descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data/authority boundaries by static inspection and by the tests as written, but did not independently re-execute these commands; the recorded exit codes are author-reported, not reviewer-reproduced. This is the expected pre-review state, not an implementation defect.
findings.recommendation: Before the deterministic runner flips the manifest to production_ready/closed, ensure it records the full validation ladder passing and updates adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, command-evidence.json, and claude-review-result.json out of PENDING using this receipt. No code change is required for the descriptor implementation.
